skill.name: web-performance-optimization
skill.description: Optimize website and web application performance including loading speed, Core Web Vitals, bundle size, caching strategies, and runtime performance
skill.summary: Systematically optimize web performance using Core Web Vitals, code splitting, image optimization, and caching strategies
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 94 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill file is pure documentation (SKILL.md) containing markdown code examples and best practices for web performance optimization. Detected patterns such as dynamic import(), shell commands, and URLs are within markdown code blocks as educational examples, not executable code. No security risks identified.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Command Examples
low_findings.description: File contains bash commands and code examples within markdown code blocks for educational purposes only. These are not executable code and pose no security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file examination confirms all detected patterns are within markdown code examples, not executable code. The file is purely educational documentation.

content.value_statement: Slow websites hurt user experience and SEO rankings. This skill helps you systematically measure, analyze, and optimize web performance using proven techniques like code splitting, image optimization, and Core Web Vitals improvements.
actual_capabilities: Analyze Lighthouse audit results and identify performance bottlenecks
Provide specific optimization strategies for Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
Guide code splitting and lazy loading implementation for JavaScript bundles
Recommend image optimization techniques including modern formats (WebP, AVIF)
Suggest caching strategies and CDN implementation approaches
Create performance optimization checklists tailored to your application stack
limitations: Cannot run performance audits directly - relies on tools like Lighthouse
Does not automatically implement optimizations - provides guidance and code examples
Requires access to your codebase and build configuration for bundle analysis
Performance improvements depend on your specific application architecture

use_cases.title: E-commerce Site Optimization
use_cases.description: Optimize product pages to improve conversion rates by reducing load times and improving Core Web Vitals scores
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers working on e-commerce sites

use_cases.title: Dashboard Performance Improvement
use_cases.description: Reduce JavaScript bundle size and optimize data loading for faster dashboard interactions

use_cases.title: Content Site Speed Optimization
use_cases.description: Implement image optimization, lazy loading, and caching strategies for faster content delivery
use_cases.target_user: Developers optimizing blogs, news sites, and content platforms

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Performance Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: I ran a Lighthouse audit and got these results: [paste scores]. Help me understand what's causing poor performance and prioritize which issues to fix first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have Lighthouse results but need help interpreting them and creating an action plan

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Core Web Vitals
prompt_templates.prompt: My Core Web Vitals are: LCP [value], FID [value], CLS [value]. Show me specific code changes to improve these metrics for my [framework] application.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need targeted solutions for specific Core Web Vitals issues

prompt_templates.title: Reduce Bundle Size
prompt_templates.prompt: My JavaScript bundle is [size] and takes [time] to load on 3G. Analyze my dependencies and suggest code splitting strategies or smaller alternatives.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when bundle size is causing slow load times and you need reduction strategies

prompt_templates.title: Implement Image Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement an image optimization strategy for my site. I have [number] images totaling [size]. Show me how to convert to modern formats and implement lazy loading in [framework].
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a complete image optimization solution with code examples

output_examples.input: My LCP is 4.2s and I need it under 2.5s. The main hero image is 2.5MB.
output_examples.output: Convert the hero image to AVIF format (saves ~80%), compress to under 200KB, add preload link, use fetchpriority='high', and implement responsive images with srcset for different screen sizes.

output_examples.input: My JavaScript bundle is 850KB gzipped and causing slow TTI.
output_examples.output: Replace moment.js (67KB) with date-fns (12KB), import only needed lodash functions instead of entire library, implement route-based code splitting for heavy components, and defer non-critical scripts like analytics.

output_examples.input: I have a CLS score of 0.25 with content jumping around.
output_examples.output: Add width and height attributes to all images, use CSS aspect-ratio property, implement skeleton loaders for dynamic content, and reserve space for ads/widgets before they load.

best_practices: Always establish baseline metrics with Lighthouse before making optimizations to measure improvement accurately
Focus on Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) first as they directly impact user experience and SEO rankings
Test performance on real mobile devices and slow 3G networks, not just desktop with fast internet
anti_patterns: Optimizing without measuring first - always run audits to identify actual bottlenecks before making changes
Over-optimizing desktop while ignoring mobile performance - most users are on mobile devices with slower networks
Premature optimization - focus on high-impact changes that affect user-facing metrics rather than micro-optimizations

faq.question: What are Core Web Vitals and why do they matter?
faq.answer: Core Web Vitals are Google's performance metrics: LCP (loading speed), FID (interactivity), and CLS (visual stability). They directly impact SEO rankings and user experience. Good scores are LCP under 2.5s, FID under 100ms, and CLS under 0.1.

faq.question: Should I optimize images or JavaScript first?
faq.answer: Start with whichever issue your Lighthouse audit identifies as most severe. Generally, optimize images first if they're over 1MB each, as this is often the quickest win. Then address JavaScript bundle size if it's over 200KB gzipped.

faq.question: What's the difference between lazy loading and code splitting?
faq.answer: Lazy loading defers loading resources (like images) until they're needed. Code splitting breaks your JavaScript into smaller chunks loaded on demand. Use both together: lazy load images and below-the-fold components, split code by route or feature.

faq.question: How do I know if my optimizations are working?
faq.answer: Run Lighthouse audits before and after changes, documenting each metric. Test on multiple devices and network speeds. Use real user monitoring (RUM) tools to track actual user experience over time, not just lab scores.

faq.question: Can this skill implement optimizations automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expert guidance, code examples, and optimization strategies. You or your development team will implement the suggested changes. The skill helps you understand what to optimize and how to approach it.

faq.question: What tools should I use alongside this skill?
faq.answer: Lighthouse (built into Chrome DevTools) for audits, webpack-bundle-analyzer for bundle analysis, WebPageTest for detailed waterfall charts, and Chrome DevTools Performance tab for runtime profiling. This skill helps interpret results from all these tools.
